title: Funeral of Amanoa
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Battle of Onderon}} {{Battle |prev=[[Battle of Iziz]]''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon]]'' |conc= |next=[[Second Battle of Onderon]]''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising]]'' |image=[[File:1stBattleOnderon.jpg]] |name=Funeral of Amanoa |conflict=[[Freedon Nadd Uprising]][[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]] |date=[[3998 BBY]]{{FactFile|113|REV 1-2|Naddist Revolt}} |place=[[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]], capital [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] |result=[[Naddist]] victory *[[Arca Jeth]] is captured *[[King/Legends|King]] [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]] is revealed to be alive *The sarcophagi of [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Amanoa]] and [[Freedon Nadd]] are recovered |side1=[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |side2=[[Naddist]]s |side3= |side4= |commanders1=[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] Arca Jeth |commanders2=King Ommin *[[Warb Null]]† |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=1 Jedi Master *Arca Jeth 3 [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] *[[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] *[[Cay Qel-Droma]] *[[Tott Doneeta]] [[Drexl|Beast Rider war-beasts]] *[[Beast Riders]] *[[Beast Warrior Commando]]s **[[Oron Kira]] **[[Gobee]] Iziz soldiers |forces2=Naddists *1 [[Sith sorcerer]] **King Ommin [[Naddist army|Dark Side Army]] *1 [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] **Warb Null† *[[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]] **[[Novar (Human)|Novar]] *Naddist troops **[[Darksider]] battalions **[[Sith Elite Warbot|Sith war droids]] **[[Groundborer]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Unidentified number of Beast Riders *Unidentified number of Iziz soldiers |casual2=*Warb Null *Unidentified number of Naddists |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Dialogue|Gobee|RUN! They're coming!|Arca Jeth|Eh, what's that? One of the Beast Riders!|Ulic Qel-Droma|He's running this way! Telling us to…by the Force! What was that? Another explosion!|Gobee|The worshipers of Freedon Nadd are here!|attr=Gobee, Arca Jeth, and Ulic Qel-Droma|audio=Gobee-Arca-Ulic.flac|src=}} The '''Funeral of Amanoa''' was an event that took place during the [[Freedon Nadd Uprising]], in the year [[3998 BBY]], which saw the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]-empowered [[Naddist army|army]] commanded by [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]] of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]] attack the combined forces of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Beast Riders]] of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] during the [[Funeral/Legends|funeral]] procession of Ommin's wife, the [[Sith sorcerer|Sith sorceress]] [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Amanoa]], as a group of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] led by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]] prepared to transport both Amanoa's sarcophagus and that of the ancient [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Freedon Nadd]] to a newly constructed [[Tomb of Freedon Nadd|mausoleum]] on the far side of Onderon's jungle [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]]. During Amanoa's procession, a massive [[groundborer]] emerged from underground, revealing an army of [[darksider]]s led by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Warb Null]]. The army managed to retake the coffins and retreated with them on their [[Tank/Legends|war machine]] into the subterranean levels of the [[City/Legends|city]] of Iziz, where it was suspected that Amanoa's husband, the sorcerer King Ommin, resided. Arca Jeth, [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knight [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], and Amanoa's daughter, [[Galia]], followed the borer into the tunnels and discovered that Ommin, who had been previously thought to be dead by the general populace of Onderon, was indeed alive, ''and'' in league with the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of Freedon Nadd. Under orders from Nadd, Jeth was captured and taken deeper into the tunnels after a brief confrontation with Ommin and his [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith master]]. As Qel-Droma and Galia attempted to escape, Warb Null confronted them, but was swiftly defeated by the Jedi Knight. They then returned to the surface, where Qel-Droma, realizing that reinforcements were necessary, sent a distress message to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], beseeching their aid. ==Prelude== ===Funeral for the Queen=== {{Quote|It looks like we arrived on Onderon just in time for a funeral, Tott Doneeta.''"
"''Yes, Oss Wilum. Master Arca has ordered that the body of Queen Amanoa, and the remains of the Sith Lord Freedon Nadd, be removed from the planet. The Onderonians are taking it to a tomb on the fourth moon.|Oss Wilum and Tott Doneeta|audio=OssWillumAndTottDoneeta.flac|}} [[File:FuneralOfAmanoa-TFNU1.jpg|thumb|left|270px|The funeral of Amanoa.]] For nearly four hundred years, the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], had been embroiled in a [[Beast Wars|civil conflict]] between the only established city of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz's]] [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]-worshiping citizens, and the [[Beast Riders]] from the planet's wilderness. The final battle saw the intervention of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], who had dispatched the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Cay Qel-Droma|Cay]] and [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Tott Doneeta]], to mediate the civil unrest. The Knights were unsuccessful in bringing about a peaceful resolution between the [[Beast-Lord]] [[Oron Kira]] and the ruler of Iziz, [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Amanoa]]. Thus, their mission quickly degenerated into an all-out [[Battle of Iziz|battle]]. Only after the arrival of their own [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]], the [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] Jedi [[Arca Jeth]], who used his skill with Jedi [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] to sway the tide of fighting, was the battle brought to an end. As a last measure, Jeth and his Jedi confronted Amanoa, who had been secretly drawing upon the power of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Freedon Nadd]] in his [[Tomb of Freedon Nadd#Onderon tomb|tomb]] underneath the [[Iziz Royal Palace|royal palace]] of Iziz. Jeth [[Force Light|cast out]] the darkness of Nadd's tomb with the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], and without the Dark Lord's influence, Amanoa's power faded as well. She died in her daughter's arms, unable and unwilling to succumb to the light. With Amanoa dead, Iziz was left without a legitimate ruler. As was the way of the [[Iziz ruling family|Onderon Royal Family]], Amanoa's daughter, [[Galia]], then assumed the throne, and she and her new husband, Oron Kira, became the new [[King/Legends|King]] and Queen of Onderon. Immediately, they began making preparations to inter Amanoa and Freedon Nadd, whose remains had been confiscated by the Jedi when Amanoa perished. A [[Funeral/Legends|funeral]] commenced, under the direction of Arca Jeth, who was still concerned with the strong darkness he felt emanating from the coffin. To ensure that the dark side power of Nadd's sarcophagus could no longer oppress the citizens of Onderon, Jeth ordered a new tomb to be constructed of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Beskar/Legends|iron]] on [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], the planet's jungle [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. Amanoa would be laid there as well. ===Naddist ambush=== [[File:Groundborer.jpg|thumb|right|170px|The Naddists' war machine emerges from underground Iziz.]] {{Dialogue|Gobee|Orders from the Jedi. They say someone's down here practicin' Sith magic. Maybe ol' King Ommin. Maybe even Freedon Nadd himself, for all ''I'' know.|Beast Rider|King Ommin! Thought he was dead! Thought they were both dead! What are we lookin' for, a ghost?|King Ommin|If it is a ''ghost'' you seek, you've ''found'' one!|attr=Gobee and another Beast Rider, as they encounter King Ommin in the catacombs|audio=Gobee-Ommin.flac|src=}} Meanwhile, Oron Kira's second-in-command, [[Gobee]], and another Beast Rider were ordered by the Jedi to search the catacombs under Iziz, where it was rumored that practitioners of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] were still lurking in the depths. As they delved deeper through miles of tunnels, Gobee and his partner came upon none other than King [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]] himself. The Queen's procession was interrupted by Gobee and the other Beast Rider when they emerged from the tunnels, moments ahead of an attack. Before they could warn their fellow Beast Riders and the Jedi, the sound of a massive [[Groundborer|boring]] [[Tank/Legends|machine]] interrupted them. More than just a tunnel-maker, the war engine protruded and sprang forth from below Iziz, releasing scores of [[Naddist]] soldiers. At their front was a gigantic [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-wielding [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], whose [[Sith trooper armor/Legends|armor]] radiated with the power of Sith magic. ==The battle== ===Duel with a Dark Jedi=== [[File:Warbnullfight.jpg|thumb|left|170px|Jedi Knights duel with Warb Null.]] {{Quote|Come'on Oss Wilum! If you came for battle…''"
"''For battle, come to ''Me''!''"
"''By the Force! it's huge!''"
"''I am Warb Null, Guardian of Freedon Nadd!|Cay Qel-Droma, upon sight of Warb Null|audio=Cay-WarbNull.flac|}} Immediately, Oron Kira and his men engaged the Naddists as they emptied their war machine, while the Knights engaged the Dark Jedi, who identified himself as [[Warb Null]]. Before he could join the battle, Arca Jeth was suddenly attacked through the Force by an unknown adversary and incapacitated. Meanwhile, Null's battle with the Jedi provided ample distraction for his Naddist soldiers, who were tasked with recapturing the sarcophagi of Nadd and Amanoa and bringing them aboard the war machine. Successful in their mission, Null then called for a retreat; the tunnel-maker pulled back and descended into the depths, with the Dark Jedi following close behind. Ulic Qel-Droma chased after them, grabbing onto the tunnel-maker and demanding that the armored Null return and face him. Null never came out, however, and Qel-Droma was nearly pulled into the gaping maw left behind in the war machine's wake. He jumped off the machine in time to avoid falling in the hole after it and rejoined his comrades, just as they were tending to the felled Arca Jeth. Meanwhile, Tott Doneeta received a message that another contingent of the darksider army was approaching the city. Jeth had barely recovered from the dark side attack when he realized that the Naddists were only distracting the Jedi; their real intention was to recover the sarcophagi of Amanoa and Freedon Nadd. Jeth had deduced that Nadd's coffin was the source of the dark power plaguing Onderon, and that retrieving it along with Amanoa's was the only course of action. It was then that Galia revealed the fact that her father, Ommin, was indeed alive, and that he may be the Jedi's only hope in recovering the sarcophagi. Tott Doneeta, [[Oss Wilum]], and Cay Qel-Droma remained behind with Oron Kira, while Arca Jeth and Ulic Qel-Droma planned to follow the war machine's exit-tunnel, in hopes of confronting the darksiders. Galia insisted on going with them, due to her familiarity with the catacombs. The trio descended into the darkness of the tunnels, intent on finding the darksiders, the sarcophagi, and possibly even King Ommin. ===Enter King Ommin=== [[File:ArcaNadd.jpg|thumb|right|140px|Arca Jeth confronts the shadow of Freedon Nadd.]] {{Dialogue|Ulic Qel-Droma|Dark side or not, when I meet that warrior Warb Null I'm going to—|Ommin|He is not your worst enemy, Ulic Qel-Droma!|Ulic Qel-Droma|Who is that?|Arca Jeth|King Ommin.|attr=Ulic Qel-Droma, Arca Jeth, and King Ommin|audio=Ulic-Ommin-Arca.flac|src=}} Galia found that, as they traveled deeper underground, she was less familiar with the catacombs than she first believed. Jeth, however, felt the potency of the dark side much stronger there; he was sure that they were indeed going in the right direction. Ulic Qel-Droma was not so concerned with where they were going or finding the King as he was with confronting Warb Null. It was not long, however, before the trio stumbled upon King Ommin himself, who was being physically supported by a full-body, [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] [[Ommin's mechanical exoskeleton|exoskeleton]]. Ommin told them that his support-[[Cybernetics/Legends|frame]] was required to augment his movements, but Jeth sensed that a darker power was at work. Ommin bid him close, as he revealed his allegiance with the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of Freedon Nadd, whom he had summoned personally from [[Chaos/Legends|Chaos]]. Freedon Nadd then appeared behind Ommin, also empowering him with the dark side of the Force. Arca Jeth immediately confronted Nadd but was intercepted by Ommin, who seized Jeth's throat and commenced smothering him with waves of dark side energy. Jeth was incapacitated, and Qel-Droma attempted to rescue him, but he was unable to penetrate the energy field in which Jeth was encased. Ommin then revealed his [[Naddist army|Dark Side Army]]: fully armed, ready for battle, and led by the armor-clad Warb Null. With the Dark Jedi at his flank, the King attempted to seduce Galia to the dark side. He was promptly refused by his daughter, whose only wish was to escape. Qel-Droma, however, gave over to his desire for revenge; he cut his way through Ommin's warriors to Warb Null, and as the King and Freedon Nadd descended into the depths with a subdued Jeth, Qel-Droma engaged the armored darksider in [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. ===Death of Warb Null=== {{Quote|My arm! You've cut it off! The pain…mercy I beg!''"
"''I'll give you mercy—the same mercy you showed my brother!|Warb Null and Ulic Qel-Droma, as Qel-Droma beheads Null|audio=Ulic-Warb_Null.flac|}} Qel-Droma and Null's duel was brief but intense, and Null was overpowered by the enraged Jedi. Qel-Droma parried several blows before dodging an overhand slash completely and flipping over Null, landing behind him and gaining the advantage as well. Qel-Droma instantly attacked by [[Cho sun/Legends|severing]] Null's weapon arm, leaving him downed, humbled, and begging for amnesty. Qel-Droma lent no ear as he brought his lightsaber around and [[Sai cha/Legends|beheaded]] the Dark Jedi. Unbeknownst to him, the [[Death/Legends|death]] of Null had been planned by Nadd, who saw the duel as a test of the young Jedi's growing dark side power. It was only then that Qel-Droma realized that he had forsaken both Galia and Jeth, the latter of which had been kidnapped by Ommin and Nadd in the midst of the duel. In their place was Ommin's Dark Side Army, blocking them from the tunnel in which Ommin had departed. Realizing that he was no match for an entire army of Sith magic-empowered warriors, Qel-Droma quickly fled with Galia through the tunnels toward the surface. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|If the dark side could take Master Arca, then it's ''too strong'' for us. We're going to need ''more Jedi Knights''—and the ''forces of the Republic''!|Ulic Qel-Droma|[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 1]]}} [[File:EscapeToFortressKira.jpg|thumb|left|280px|Oron Kira leads the Jedi to Fortress Kira.]] Galia and Qel-Droma emerged above ground, only to find that the Royal Palace as well as the city had been retaken by the Naddists. They wandered the war-torn streets of Iziz until they happened upon Cay Qel-Droma, Oss Wilum, and Oron Kira. Ulic Qel-Droma explained to them that Jeth had been kidnapped, and that another of Ommin's dark side militias was approaching fast to join their colleagues in battle. It was then decided that Galia would be ferried away to safety, while the Jedi and Oron Kira would take up command from the Beast Rider stronghold, [[Kira Fortress|Fortress Kira]]. Ulic Qel-Droma knew that the situation had deteriorated further into something that he and his current strength in numbers were incapable of handling. Therefore, his only recourse was to relay a message to the Jedi Order, and also the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], with hopes that they would both dispatch reinforcements. Qel-Droma's message was indeed received and debated over by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Ministry of Defense]], who decided to send the entire [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] in defense of Onderon. The Jedi too acknowledged the call for aid, and responded to their fellows by sending five Knights to aid them. One of the chosen Jedi, a young woman named [[Nomi Sunrider]], was also selected to lead the Republic [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet]] into battle. These things were unknown to Qel-Droma and his companions on Onderon, however, leaving them with nothing other than hope to rely on. ==Behind the scenes== The battle during the funeral first appeared in the issue [[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1|''Freedon Nadd Uprising 1'']] of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] series of comics. The battle was conceived and written by author [[Tom Veitch]], and was drawn by artist [[Tony Akins]]. Both Veitch and [[Kevin J. Anderson]] would use the events portrayed in this battle and the [[Second Battle of Onderon|following battle]] as background details for their joint venture [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Dark Lords of the Sith'']], where such characters as Ulic and Cay Qel-Droma would be explored in depth while playing integral parts to the story line. The battle also provided a backdrop for the [[Krath Holy Crusade]], which was written as a part of the larger [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. It was later recounted in the [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]], written by [[John Whitman]] and based on the original script by Veitch. In the original comic appearance of the battle, Queen Galia is reunited with Oron Kira and also accompanies him to Fortress Kira with the Jedi. In the audio version, however, Galia is taken to safety by Tott Doneeta, while Oron Kira and the rest of the Jedi take up command from Fortress Kira. Also, in the audio drama, Warb Null loses his arm to Ulic Qel-Droma in their duel before being killed, whereas in the comic version he is simply killed. Another note of contradiction occurs when Ulic Qel-Droma blames Warb Null for cleaving Cay Qel-Droma's arm, causing him to show no mercy to Null before killing him. This conflicts with both the comic version and the audio drama, where in the former, Cay's arm is severed by Amanoa's royal guards, and in the latter, his arm is blasted off by Novar. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]] {{Mo}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]] {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Codex}} *{{InsiderCite|147|Hammer (short story)|reprint=1}} {{Vision|[[Malleus|Telloti Cillmam'n]]}} ==Sources== *''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' *''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' *{{FactFile|113|REV 1-2|Naddist Revolt}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *{{DB|character|ulicqeldroma|Qel-Droma, Ulic}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Erste Schlacht von Onderon |pl=Pierwsza bitwa o Onderon (3998 BBY) }} [[Category:Battles of the Freedon Nadd Uprising]]
